rulings.Case Title: PAUL VON WIEDENFIELD ET AL VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Seal Portions Of Plaintiff Saunders? Motion To Compel Further Responses
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 5, 2026, line 1. DEFENDANT ABBOTT LABORATORIES Motion To Seal Portions Of Plaintiff Saunders Motion To Compel Further Responses. The unopposed motion to seal is granted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID BALDINI VS. NUVOLUM INC., A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Nuvolum Inc.S Response To Plaintiffs Demand For Production, Set No. Two; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 5, 2026, line 3. PLAINTIFF DAVID BALDINI'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Nuvolum Inc.'s Response To Plaintiffs Demand For Production, Set No. Two; Request For Sanctions. The unopposed motion to compel further responses to the request for production of documents, set two, is granted. Defendant's objections lack merit. Defendant shall provide amended responses within two weeks of notice of entry of this order and shall produce responsive documents. Defendant may withhold privileged documents but must provide a privilege log. Plaintiff seeks sanctions. Defendant's objections lack substantial justification. Defendant and its counsel are sanctioned $3000, payable jointly and severally within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CHRIS SCHROEDER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 5, 2026, line 6. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Hearing required. The court's tentatives for Monday are prepared before Friday's ex parte hearings. The court does not know whether Plaintiff has obtained a continuance. In the event Plaintiff obtains a continuance, the court's indicated is to continue the summary judgment motion for hearing. In the event Plaintiff fails to obtain a continuance, the court's indicated appears below. The motion for summary judgment is granted. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to the parties via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. rulings.Case Title: JAMES CALDWELL VS. PUBMATIC INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Unopposed Motion To Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 5, 2026, line 5. DEFENDANT PUBMATIC, INC.'S Motion And Unopposed Motion To Stay Proceedings. The unopposed motion to stay is granted. The court's usual practice is to fix an end date for stays. The case is stayed for all purposes through July 13, 2026. In the event the motion to dismiss in the federal action is resolved earlier, the parties may stipulate or move to lift the stay earlier. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.
